Abbreviations (Level AAA)

3.1.4 Abbreviations (Level AAA)
Understanding Success Criterion 3.1.4: Abbreviations | WAI | W3C 

Summary
Abbreviations and acronyms are not expanded on first use, causing comprehension issues for screen reader users, dyslexic users, and users unfamiliar with fisheries and internal service terminology.

Description
Multiple pages across the Create a UK Catch Certificate service use undefined acronyms and abbreviations (e.g. DAA, MMO, FAO, EoD, AMS, ECC, DA, BCP, EEZ, RFMO).

Under WCAG 3.1.4, abbreviations must be expanded on their first occurrence on the page, enabling users to understand their meaning if encountered later.

Screen reader testing (JAWS) confirms that links and headings containing unexplained acronyms are announced letter‑by‑letter or as unfamiliar terms, increasing cognitive load and reducing comprehension.

Colour‑blind and dyslexic users reported confusion and difficulty maintaining context when acronyms are not explained inline.

Affected Pages & Code References
Cookies Policy – Create a UK catch certificate - https://fes-ext.pre.private.defra.gov.uk/cookies

Privacy Notice – Create a UK catch certificate - https://fes-ext.pre.private.defra.gov.uk/privacy-notice

What are you exporting? – Create a UK catch certificate - https://fes-ext.pre.private.defra.gov.uk/create-catch-certificate/.../what-are-you-exporting

Product Favourites – Create a UK catch certificate - https://fes-ext.pre.private.defra.gov.uk/create-catch-certificate/.../manage-favourites

Notifications - https://fes-int.pre.private.defra.gov.uk/notifications

Expectation of Data Settings - https://fes-int.pre.private.defra.gov.uk/expectation-of-data-settings

AMS Operations - https://fes-int.pre.private.defra.gov.uk/ams-operations

View Documents - https://fes-int.pre.private.defra.gov.uk/documents/catchCert/...

User Impact
Screen reader users: Acronyms announced without context, reducing meaning.

Dyslexic users: Increased cognitive load and loss of page orientation.

New or infrequent users: Lack of domain knowledge makes acronyms confusing.

Compliance risk: Repeated WCAG 3.1.4 failures across core user journeys.

Expected Behaviour
On the first instance per page, acronyms and abbreviations are expanded with their full meaning.
Subsequent uses on the same page may use the abbreviated form.

Suggested Fix
HTML

<a href="WebChoices " class="govuk-link">

 Digital Advertising Alliance (DAA) WebChoices service

Other acronyms 

MMO → Marine Management Organisation (MMO)

FAO → Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO)

EoD → Expectation of Data (EoD)

AMS → Automated Monitoring System (AMS)

ECC → Export Catch Certificate (ECC)

DA → Data Availability (DA) (confirm intended meaning)

BCP → Business Continuity Plan (BCP)

EEZ → Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ)

RFMO → Regional Fisheries Management Organisation (RFMO)

Where appropriate, <abbr title=""> elements may also be used.

Acceptance Criteria
All acronyms and abbreviations are expanded on first use per page
